User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Assembleia - Mensa Brasil application. This entity stores basic profile information but no authentication credentials. Administrator status is determined by document existence in the /admins collection, not by a role field here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

email.description: The user's email address. Used for identification and communication.

avatarDataUri.description: Data URI for the user's avatar image. Can be a data:image/... string or a URL for default avatars.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

AdminMarker.title: Admin Marker
AdminMarker.description: An empty object used as a marker in the /admins collection. The existence of this document for a user's UID grants them administrator privileges.

Assembly.title: Assembly
Assembly.description: Represents a formal assembly event, managed by administrators, including details for live viewing and associated activities like polls and speaking queues.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assembly entity.

title.description: The title or name of the assembly.

description.description: A detailed description of the assembly's agenda or purpose.

date.description: The scheduled date and time of the assembly.

youtubeUrl.description: URL for the YouTube live stream embedding the assembly.

imageUrl.description: URL or Base64 Data URI for the assembly's cover image.

administratorId.description: Reference to the User (administrator) who created and manages this assembly. (Relationship: User 1:N Assembly)

status.description: The current status of the assembly.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the assembly record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the assembly's information was updated.

Poll.title: Poll
Poll.description: Represents a poll conducted during an assembly, created by an administrator, with specific questions and voting periods.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Poll entity.

assemblyId.description: Reference to the Assembly in which this poll is conducted. (Relationship: Assembly 1:N Poll)

question.description: The question posed to members for this poll.

startTime.description: The date and time when voting for this poll opens.

endTime.description: The date and time when voting for this poll closes.

status.description: The current status of the poll (e.g., 'draft', 'open', 'closed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the poll was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the poll's information was updated.

PollOption.title: PollOption
PollOption.description: Represents a specific voting option available within a poll.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PollOption entity.

pollId.description: Reference to the Poll to which this option belongs. (Relationship: Poll 1:N PollOption)

text.description: The text of the voting option (e.g., 'Sim', 'Não', 'Abstenção', or a custom answer).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the poll option was created.

Vote.title: Vote
Vote.description: Represents a single vote cast by a user on a specific poll option within a poll. This entity supports transparent voting by linking the user to their chosen option.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vote entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who cast this vote. (Relationship: User 1:N Vote)

pollId.description: Reference to the Poll for which this vote was cast. (Relationship: Poll 1:N Vote)

pollOptionId.description: Reference to the PollOption chosen by the user. (Relationship: PollOption 1:N Vote)

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the vote was cast.

SpeakerQueueItem.title: SpeakerQueueItem
SpeakerQueueItem.description: Represents a request from a member to speak during an assembly, managed in a queue by administrators, potentially including a Zoom link for participation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SpeakerQueueItem entity.

assemblyId.description: Reference to the Assembly for which the user requested to speak. (Relationship: Assembly 1:N SpeakerQueueItem)

userId.description: Reference to the User who requested to speak. (Relationship: User 1:N SpeakerQueueItem)

requestTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the user requested to speak.

status.description: The current status of the speaker's request (e.g., 'requested', 'queued', 'speaking', 'completed', 'cancelled').

zoomLink.description: An optional unique Zoom link provided to the user when it's their turn to speak.

assignedTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the speaker was assigned a speaking slot or provided a Zoom link.

order.description: The sequential position of the user in the speaking queue, managed by administrators.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Users can manage their own profile. The 'id' field of the document should match the 'userId' parameter. Administrator status is managed in the separate '/admins' collection.

Identifies users who have administrator privileges. The existence of a document at this path for a given user ID signifies their admin status. Adheres to the 'Existence over Content' pattern for DBAC and Authorization Independence. Only trusted services should write to this collection.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching 'request.auth.uid' for admin checks.

definition.description: Collection for all assembly events. Includes 'administratorId' for ownership and 'status' for controlling member read access. This enables QAPs for members to list scheduled/live assemblies. All subcollections will denormalize 'administratorId' and 'status' for Authorization Independence.

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier for an assembly.

definition.description: Subcollection for polls associated with a specific assembly. Includes denormalized 'administratorId' (from parent Assembly) and 'assemblyStatus' (from parent Assembly) for Authorization Independence. Also includes its own 'status' for controlling member read/vote access (QAPs).

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent assembly.

params.name: pollId
params.description: The unique identifier for a poll within the assembly.

definition.description: Subcollection for voting options within a specific poll. Includes denormalized 'administratorId' (from Assembly), 'assemblyStatus' (from Assembly), and 'pollStatus' (from parent Poll) for Authorization Independence, enabling QAPs for members to view options for active polls.

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent assembly.

params.name: pollId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent poll.

params.name: optionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a poll option.

definition.description: Subcollection for individual votes cast on a poll. Includes denormalized 'administratorId' (from Assembly), 'assemblyStatus' (from Assembly), 'pollStatus' (from parent Poll), and 'userId' (the voter) for Authorization Independence. Enables QAPs for transparent voting (members can list votes only if 'pollStatus' is 'closed') and prevents multiple votes by the same user.

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent assembly.

params.name: pollId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent poll.

params.name: voteId
params.description: The unique identifier for a vote. Typically 'request.auth.uid' to ensure one vote per user per poll.

definition.description: Subcollection for speaker requests within an assembly. Includes denormalized 'administratorId' (from Assembly), 'assemblyStatus' (from Assembly), and 'userId' (the requester) for Authorization Independence. Enables QAPs for members to view the queue (order, userId, status) while restricting sensitive info like 'zoomLink' to administrators or the requesting user.

params.name: assemblyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent assembly.

params.name: queueItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a speaker queue item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, emphasizing robust and debuggable security rules. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Each subcollection document (`Poll`, `PollOption`, `Vote`, `SpeakerQueueItem`) denormalizes critical authorization context from its parent `Assembly` and `Poll` entities. Specifically, the `administratorId` of the parent `Assembly` and the `status` fields (`assemblyStatus` and `pollStatus`) are copied down to the child documents. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent documents for authorization, making rule evaluation atomic, faster, and easier to debug. For instance, a `Poll` document will contain its `administratorId` and `assemblyStatus`, allowing rules to determine admin write access or member read access (e.g., if the assembly is `live` or `scheduled`) directly from the `Poll` document itself.

**Support for Query-Aware Permissions (QAPs):**
1.  **`/users/{userId}`:** Intended for individual profile reads/writes by the user themselves. The `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}`. Listing of all users is not explicitly a requirement but could be enabled for administrators or for public profiles if needed, without additional filtering needs.
2.  **`/admins/{userId}`:** This collection adheres to the 'Existence over Content' principle for global roles. An administrator is identified by the mere existence of a document at `/admins/{request.auth.uid}`. This allows rules to check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admins/$(request.auth.uid))` for admin privileges without relying on a `role` field within a user's own mutable profile, ensuring robust DBAC.
3.  **`/assemblies/{assemblyId}`:** Members can securely `list` assemblies. Security rules will filter based on the `status` field (e.g., allowing reads only if `resource.data.status` is 'scheduled' or 'live' for non-admin users). Administrators, identified by the `/admins/{userId}` collection, can `list` and manage all assemblies regardless of status.
4.  **`/assemblies/{assemblyId}/polls/{pollId}` and `/assemblies/{assemblyId}/polls/{pollId}/options/{optionId}`:** Members can `list` polls and their options within a specific assembly. Rules enforce that this is only possible if the denormalized `assemblyStatus` indicates the assembly is active (e.g., `live` or `scheduled`) and the `pollStatus` is `open` or `closed` (depending on whether voting is allowed or results are visible). Administrators can manage all polls and options based on the denormalized `administratorId`.
5.  **`/assemblies/{assemblyId}/polls/{pollId}/votes/{voteId}`:** This structure explicitly supports 'Transparent Voting' as a QAP. Members can `list` votes *only* if the denormalized `pollStatus` is 'closed', revealing results after voting concludes. Individual users can create their own vote (`request.auth.uid == request.resource.data.userId`).
6.  **`/assemblies/{assemblyId}/speakerQueue/{queueItemId}`:** Members can `list` items in the queue to see their position and potentially others' `userId` and `status` (e.g., `queued`), but sensitive fields like `zoomLink` for other users would be restricted via rules to only the item's `userId` or the `administratorId`. This allows public visibility of the queue order while protecting private information.

This design prioritizes secure, efficient, and maintainable Firestore security rules by explicitly embedding authorization data where it's needed, segregating data by access pattern, and using predictable, consistent pathing and naming conventions. Each path clearly defines its security posture.
